name: Rocket_Mech

infobox.Systems: Rocket Artillery(Ranged)EffectFires a pushing artillery and creates Smoke behind the shooter.(2 damage)Upgrade I+1 Damage ()Increases damage by 1.Upgrade II+1 Damage ()Increases damage by 1.Storm Generator(Passive)EffectAll Smoke deals damage to enemy units every turn.(1 damage)Upgrade I+1 Damage ()Increases Smoke damage by 1.

sections.Team: The mech is available as part of theRusting Hulkssquad.
sections.Strategy:
Boasting a powerful weapon in Rocket Artillery, which fires a pushing projectile and drops damagingElectric Smokebehind it, makes the Rocket Mech a particularly effective front-line Ranged Mech. It is conditionally capable of neutralizing two threats at once thanks to the Smoke it leaves behind; as long as there is space, it's possible to cancel one enemy attack with the Smoke by firing in the opposite direction, while hitting another enemy with the projectile.
The versatile utility ofSmokecanceling attacks along with a starting damage of 2 makes the Rocket Mech effective in most Squads it is placed on. Regardless of where it is placed though, consider increasing its movement as soon as possible, in order to let the Mech fire from as many positions as possible.
For the other upgrades, the choice of whether to power the damage of Rocket Artillery or Storm Generator first is preference and Squad dependent. Consider Storm Generator first if playing with the Rusting Hulks ― latest before the 3rd island ― or aCustom Squadwith theSmog Mech; it's expensive at 3 Reactor Cores, but it's valuable as it will effectively raise the damage output of the entire Squad. Otherwise, prioritize the damage of the rocket to ensure that the Rocket Mech kills its target; particularly if the next island hasAlpha DiggersorTumblebugs, which the Rocket Mech's damage is effective at taking out without being quite as hampered by these enemies Rock obstacles.
